title: Sesame Pork Rice Bowls with Pickled Cucumbers
description: EveryPlate's most popular recipe — savory ground pork seasoned with soy, ginger, and sesame oil, served over fluffy rice with quick-pickled cucumbers and a drizzle of sweet chili sauce. Fast, flavorful, and endlessly repeatable.
instructions: Cook rice according to package directions. Keep warm.
Make quick-pickled cucumbers: thinly slice cucumber and toss with rice vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt. Set aside for at least 10 minutes.
Slice scallions, keeping whites and greens separate. Mince garlic and ginger.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground pork and cook, breaking it up, for 5–6 minutes until browned and cooked through.
Add scallion whites, garlic, and ginger; cook 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Add soy sauce and 1 tbsp sesame oil; toss to coat. Cook 1 more minute. Remove from heat.
Divide rice into bowls. Top with sesame pork and pickled cucumbers.
Drizzle sweet chili sauce and remaining sesame oil over each bowl. Garnish with scallion greens and sesame seeds.
tips: The quick-pickled cucumbers are what make this dish — don't skip them. They add a bright, tangy contrast to the rich pork.
Ground chicken or turkey works equally well in place of pork.
Add a soft-boiled egg on top for extra richness.
For more heat, add a squirt of sriracha or a pinch of red pepper flakes to the pork.
